title: tephrocactus setiger subsp. deminutus
intro: Tephrocactus setiger subsp. deminutus is a rare, globular cactus species found in the southwestern regions of North America, characterized by its short, woolly stems and small, yellow flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Tephrocactus setiger subsp. deminutus include wound healing, pain relief, and immune system support. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into infusions, tinctures, or salves, and are used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
